normalized_title: Riseley Activity Centre Structure Plan (City of Melville)
version_date: March 2015 (adopted by Council 17 June 2014; Modifications 1 and 2 adopted by Council 5 March 2015)
scope_summary: Structure plan for the Riseley Activity Centre, a district centre in Applecross and Ardross (Riseley Street / Canning Highway / Kearns Crescent area), City of Melville, prepared under the City of Melville Community Planning Scheme No. 5. Part One (statutory) divides the centre into five precincts (Riseley Core, Canning Corridor, The Crescent, Transitional Frame, Residential Frame) and sets binding land use permissibility, retail floorspace, residential density, building height and setback standards that vary the Scheme. Part Two (non-statutory) provides explanatory context on movement, urban form, activity and implementation.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Precincts 1, 2 and 3 (Riseley Core, Canning Corridor, The Crescent)
Precincts 4 and 5 (Transitional Frame, Residential Frame), per Residential Design Codes

key_numeric_standards.topic: site cover / plot ratio / open space
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: whole structure plan area unless otherwise specified

key_numeric_standards.topic: private open space (terrace/balcony/courtyard connected to living space)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: terrace, balcony or courtyard, all precincts

key_numeric_standards.unit: m minimum width (proportionate ceding at subdivision/development)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: lots adjacent to a right of way
all precincts; heights distributed by map
ground floor commercial tenancies, Precincts 1, 2 and 3
first 2 storeys to public streets, Precincts 1 and 2 (and Kearns Crescent in Precinct 3), unless exceptions apply

key_numeric_standards.value: nil up to 5 storeys; min 1.5 m above 5 storeys

key_numeric_standards.topic: setback to adjoining properties (development up to 3 storeys)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: adjoining properties outside Precinct 2; similar standard in Precinct 4 and outside structure plan area

key_numeric_standards.topic: setback to adjoining properties (development over 3 storeys)
setback to adjoining properties (development over 2 storeys)
rear setback to adjoining properties outside structure plan area (over 2 storeys)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: Precinct 5 / edges of structure plan area

key_numeric_standards.unit: m from building line of 3rd storey
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: any level above 3 storeys facing a gazetted street (Precinct 4); 3 m where facing Kearns Crescent in Precinct 3
roof plant screening and unenclosed roof structures excluded from storey count

definitions: storey (cl 3.8, including mezzanine note and roof-structure exclusions)
P - use permitted by the Scheme subject to compliance (cl 7.2)
D - use not permitted unless discretion exercised by planning approval (cl 7.2)
A - use not permitted unless approval granted after special notice (cl 7.2)
X - use not permitted by the Scheme (cl 7.2)
other words and expressions take meanings given in City of Melville Community Planning Scheme No. 5 (cl 3.1)

cross_references.cited: City of Melville Community Planning Scheme No. 5
State Planning Policy 4.2 - Activity Centres for Perth and Peel
Local Commercial and Activity Centres Strategy (City of Melville)
Crime Prevention Through Environmental Design of Buildings Local Planning Policy
Energy Efficiency in Building Design Local Planning Policy

quality_flags: Two-column PDF layout interleaved during extraction: many lines merge fragments from adjacent columns, so clause text frequently reads out of order and needs careful reassembly
Cover date OCR artifact 'MAJRUCLHY 22001154' is the interleaving of 'MARCH 2015' and 'JULY 2014'

Extracted text contains cover pages, executive summary and Part One (Statutory Section, clauses 1-10.41, internal pages 1-8) only; Part Two (Non-Statutory Explanatory Section, contents pages 11-51) and Appendices A-B listed in the table of contents are absent - the source PDF appears to be the 16-page statutory extract
Structure Plan Map (Plan 1) rendered as fragmented single-character vertical text; precinct/height map content is not reliable from text alone (R50 and 3-6 storey height labels visible)
Mojibake encoding artifacts present (e.g. 'a€“' sequences for en-dash)
